evidence: We use, develop, and incorporate within our technology platform and services, systems and tools that incorporate AI and machine learning, including generative AI.
New technologies have required, and could require us in the future, to spend more to modify or adapt our products to attract and retain clients

we strive to establish and maintain appropriate governance and risk management processes
we seek to mitigate by relying on proprietary or walled-garden environments to enhance data protection and operational controls

The E.U. Artificial Intelligence Act...poses further challenges for organizations in managing transparency, fairness, and accountability for AI use.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-specific spend, headcount, or named vendor/cloud partnerships disclosed in the excerpts; generic language about technology investment only.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Named use cases (generative AI, ML in technology platform) are mentioned but no deployment status metrics or quantified business results are provided; some language is aspirational.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section and MD&A material is present with named, company-specific risk mechanisms (vendor dependence, regulatory compliance costs, algorithm bias, cybersecurity via AI-enabled phishing), justifying score 4; no quantified risk figures or named oversight governance bodies are disclosed, precluding score 5.
